| TQ28 | Three-way merge on source toggle-back                                                         | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by init\_spike A2+A3.** When user is in source mode and agent writes via DirectConnection, both edits must survive toggle-back. Architecture: snapshot Y.Doc state on toggle-to-source. On toggle-back: diff snapshot vs user's source edits to find human changes. Detect agent-added blocks (positions beyond snapshot length). Merge: human edits applied, agent additions preserved, conflicts → human wins. Combined with A1 (agent markdown write path): agent writes are visible in source mode in real-time (Y.Doc observer → re-serialize → push to CodeMirror) AND preserved on toggle-back. See init\_spike/src/editor/three-way-merge.ts. | TQ27 | MDX component scope: predefined registry, no imports                                          | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | **Validated by real content analysis.** agents-docs: 201 .mdx files, 846 JSX component uses, 3 top-level imports (1 build-time templating, 1 one-off custom component, 1 redundant). openbolts docs: 8 .mdx files, 2 Callout uses, zero imports. Components are resolved globally via getMDXComponents() — not imported per-file. The predefined set: \~20 components (Callout, Tabs/Tab, Steps/Step, Card/Cards, Accordion, AutoTypeTable, Image, CodeGroup, Video, Warning, Note, Tip, Files/File, Banner, SkillRule, ComparisonTable, Snippet, APIPage). All have simple string/boolean props. No expression props, no inline JSX expressions, no MDX comments in real content. This means observer sync (TQ25) handles all actual MDX patterns — the void node approach covers the real usage. See /reports/mdx-crdt-roundtrip-fidelity/ addendum Section 11. | TQ29 | Component discovery: layered scan (built-in → project → explicit config)                      | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Three-layer discovery hierarchy for the component registry. **Layer 0 (built-in):** openknowledge ships default components (Callout, Tabs, Accordion, Card, Steps, Note/Warning/Tip) — always available, zero config. **Layer 1 (project auto-detection):** At dev server start, scan: (1) `mdx-components.tsx` (fumadocs convention) → parse imports, merge; (2) `src/components/` → scan .tsx files; (3) `.ok/components/` → scan. **Layer 2 (explicit config):** `.ok/components.ts` for manual mapping + overrides. Open sub-questions: Should auto-detection be convention-based or config-driven? How to handle fumadocs-ui library components in node\_modules (too expensive to scan all, must be explicit via mdx-components.tsx)? Layer priority when same component name appears in multiple layers (project overrides built-in)? Spike validates Layer 0 + simple scan of `src/components/`; Layers 1-2 are P0. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

Contains: `components.ts` (explicit component registry + overrides), `component-cache.json` (react-docgen-typescript cache), future: `permissions.yaml`, `config.ts`. Analogous to `.vscode/`, `.cursor/`, `.github/`. Open sub-questions: Should this be `.ok/` or configurable? Gitignore story (config committed, cache ignored)? Options: (A) built-ins only — ship with openknowledge, user writes `jsx-component` fenced code blocks, no React project needed. (B) `.ok/components/` directory — user drops .tsx files, editor discovers them, requires React/TypeScript. (C) shadcn-style install — `npx openknowledge add callout` copies component source into project, connects to S-L7 (shadcn registry). | TQ31 | react-docgen-typescript integration: config and gotchas                                       | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Findings from typed-component-nodes spec investigation: (1) `children` prop FILTERED OUT by default — must set `skipChildrenPropWithoutDoc: false`. (2) ReactNode type appears as `"ReactNode"` or `"React.ReactNode"` depending on import — detection must check both. (3) Use `shouldExtractLiteralValuesFromEnum: true` for union→dropdown, NOT `shouldExtractValuesFromUnion: true` (explodes ReactNode into 11 useless members). (4) \~10-15s for 75 components — cache to `.ok/component-cache.json` with mtime invalidation. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/evidence/react-docgen-typescript-behavior.md. | XQ4  | Deterministic health layer — validators in core service, consumed by writes + editor + skills | Cross-cutting | P0       | Open                        | The product should compute deterministic health signals (orphan pages, broken links, frontmatter completeness, staleness, tag duplicates, stub pages) as an in-process validation library in the core service. Two consumption paths, same code: (1) **on every write** — per-file validators run inline with the MCP write pipeline, return warnings in tool response (\~1-5ms, don't block the write); (2) **batch health summary** — computed file (e.g., `.ok/health.json`, gitignored) with KB-wide signals, readable by any consumer. Editor shows health signals visually (orphan badges, broken-link underlines, stale indicators) without any skill running. The `/lint` reference skill (PQ14) EXTENDS this layer with LLM-powered checks (contradictions, missing cross-refs, concept gaps) — it reads the health summary first, then adds semantic analysis. Deterministic checks: orphans (from backlink index), broken links (from file tree), frontmatter completeness (structural parse), staleness (timestamp comparison of `compiled_at` vs latest timeline entry), tag fuzzy-matching, stub detection. LLM checks (skill-only): contradictions, missing cross-references, concept-without-page, semantic staleness, data gaps. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d11-reference-skill-mapping.md for the merged lint checklist from Karpathy + GBrain + obsidian-mind.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PQ19 | Compiled truth + timeline: content convention for agent-authored entries                      | Product       | P0       | Open                        | A convention (not product-enforced schema) for how reference skills author knowledge entries. Above-the-line: rewritable compiled truth (Key Judgment, State, Assessment, Open Questions). Below-the-line (after `---`): append-only timeline with structured entries (`- **YYYY-MM-DD** \| source \| @author — Summary. Confidence: level.`). Convention validated across 6 independent domains (intelligence analysis, Wikipedia, Zettelkasten, Karpathy, ByteRover, GBrain). Compilation rules (for skills): compiled truth always rewritten from timeline evidence (not from prior compiled truth — prevents circular compilation); timeline always appended, never rewritten; 5 metadata fields per timeline entry (date, source, author, type, confidence). Staleness: product can deterministically flag entries where `compiled_at` < latest timeline date (feeds XQ4 health layer). Resolve exact format at skill spec time. See /reports/compiled-truth-timeline-content-conventions/ D6.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| XQ5  | Git scaling: \~5K file soft ceiling for markdown+git KBs                                      | Cross-cutting | P0       | Assumed (Medium confidence) | GBrain's spec documents git performance degradation at 7,471 files / 2.3GB (Garry Tan's personal brain). Not a hard limit — Linux kernel has 80K+ files in git — but depends on commit frequency, file sizes, and working-tree operations. Open-knowledge's auto-commit pipeline (CC2: every 30-60s) amplifies the concern. P0 target (100-1000 articles) is comfortably within range. Scaling path: multi-KB (separate git repos per knowledge domain), not single-monolithic-KB. SQLite-backed storage (GBrain's approach) is a Later alternative for power users at scale. Document the limit in day-0 user-facing docs. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d6-garrytan-gbrain.md. Design space: (A) third mode alongside WYSIWYG and source, (B) split-pane alongside CodeMirror source view, (C) always-available side panel in any mode. Obsidian has reading view + source side-by-side. For MDX: requires runtime compilation — DynamicCodeBlock handles syntax highlighting, but full component rendering needs React in the preview pane. Connects to CC3 (rendering paths not yet designed), S2 (source toggle forward connections). | PQ21 | Skill auto-registration mechanics — cross-platform                                            | Product       | P0       | Open                        | When a user runs `npx openknowledge init`, how do reference skills become available to their agent? Must work cross-platform (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Cowork). Options: (A) MCP `instructions` field lists available skills on connect — agent discovers via MCP, (B) AGENTS.md in project root documents skills — any agent reads file, (C) modify agent config (e.g., `.claude/settings.json`) to register skill commands — Claude Code only, not portable, (D) skills are just .md files in the project — agent finds via catalog files (CC6), no registration needed. Option D is simplest and most portable but requires agents to know the SKILL.md convention. Option A uses existing MCP infrastructure. CC7 principle: prefer MCP/files over hooks. Connects to PQ14 (distribution), CC7 (agent DevX), CC5 (onboarding). | PQ22 | Raw artifact lifecycle — where do ingested sources land?                                      | Product       | P0       | Open                        | When an agent ingests a source (URL, PDF, pasted text), where does the raw artifact live? Options: (A) `sources/` or `raw/` convention folder — raw artifacts preserved, compiled articles reference them, (B) raw artifacts are transient — skill processes source and only the compiled article persists, (C) raw artifacts in frontmatter/metadata — source URL and extraction date recorded, raw text discarded. Rabbit hole #4 caution applies (don't over-engineer conventions — let them emerge). The compiled truth + timeline convention (PQ19) covers the *output* format; this covers the *input* lifecycle. Key question: do raw artifacts have independent value (searchable, browsable) or are they only useful during compilation? Connects to PQ6 (ingest), PQ13 (Karpathy workflow), PQ14 (reference skills). | PQ23 | Multi-project support — switching between KBs                                                 | Product       | P0       | Open                        | How does the editor handle multiple knowledge bases? Options: (A) one instance per project — user runs `npx openknowledge` in each project directory, switches via browser tabs, (B) project switcher in UI — editor lists known projects, user selects, server switches context, (C) workspace model — multiple projects open simultaneously with a sidebar per project. P0 is likely (A) — one instance, one project. Project switcher (B) is a Next feature. The MCP server already scopes to one project. XQ5 mentions "multi-KB (separate git repos)" as the scaling path. Connects to CC5 (onboarding), CC3 (web UI shell), XQ6 (deployment model).
