| PQ16 | Merge conflict approach when applying drafts                                                  | Product       | P0       | Open                        | Three options: (A) prevent conflicts — warn when human edits a file that's in a draft, (B) section-level visual merge — show "your version / draft version / keep both" per-section, (C) main wins — human's edits preserved, draft's conflicting sections shown as "couldn't apply, view draft's version." Option C is simplest for P0 (no merge UI needed, human's work preserved, agent retries). Options B needed for teams (Later).                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| TQ16 | Agent edit patterns: section-level, not character-level                                       | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Research confirmed: agents apply changes via exactly two operations — full file write and string replacement. Both section-level. 8/11 agents use string replacement as primary. Claude Code enforces read-before-write and exact match. This is factual — affects TQ13 (CRDT merge granularity question). See /reports/ai-coding-agent-tool-surfaces/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| TQ17 | JS regex on CRDT content: 2-8ms at 1000 files (faster than ripgrep)                           | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual. V8 Irregexp JIT-compiled regex on in-memory strings: 2-8ms for 1000 files / 5MB. ripgrep from disk: 22ms (bottlenecked by process spawn + I/O). At our scale, in-memory JS regex is FASTER than ripgrep because ripgrep's advantages (SIMD, parallelism, mmap) solve I/O problems that don't exist when data is in CRDT memory. No WASM, no workers needed. Pre-split lines on CRDT update is the one optimization that matters (1.2-1.7x). Bun vs Node: no consistent regex winner. See /reports/orama-vs-ripgrep-indexed-grep/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| TQ18 | Index files and regex are complementary, not competing                                        | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual, updated. P0 navigation: walkable index.md files for orientation/discovery (find articles ABOUT a topic via hierarchical browsing). Regex for exact pattern matching (find lines CONTAINING a pattern). At 100-1000 files, no search engine needed — index files + JS regex on CRDT content (2-8ms) cover both agent needs. BM25/vector search deferred to Next. For agents: index files serve Layers 1-2 (orientation + discovery), grep serves Layer 3 (targeted extraction). Research: /reports/orama-vs-ripgrep-indexed-grep/ D7, /reports/kb-index-navigation-patterns-for-agents/ D9.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ19 | structuredContent not viable for enrichment (most hosts ignore it)                            | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual. MCP structuredContent (added June 2025 spec) is ignored by Claude Code (issue #4427, closed "not planned"), Cursor (confirmed by team), Windsurf, n8n. Only ChatGPT Apps reads it. Enrichment MUST be in the text `content` field. Our command implementations embed metadata directly in string output (frontmatter already in files, backlinks as footer, titles/tags inline with grep results). No dual-format responses needed. See research on structuredContent support. | TQ20 | Git library: simple-git — **validated by init-spike V5**                                      | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **Validated by init-spike V5.** simple-git .raw() for git plumbing (write-tree, commit-tree, update-ref) works as expected. Isolated git index via GIT\_INDEX\_FILE env var prevents polluting developer staging area. Subprocess spawn negligible at 30s commit intervals. See init\_spike/src/server/persistence.ts. | TQ21 | just-bash as potential MCP implementation layer                                               | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Vercel Labs' just-bash: TypeScript virtual bash with pluggable IFileSystem. 100+ Unix commands reimplemented. Mintlify ChromaFs built on it (30K+ daily conversations, \~100ms p90). A YjsFileSystem backend is viable (\~8 methods). Enables: `mcp__openkb__exec(command)` as single MCP tool, whitelisted commands, composable pipelines, enriched output. Pattern validated by just-bash-mcp (community). Connects to XQ1 Approach B. Not yet decided — open product/technical question. See /reports/just-bash-virtual-filesystem-analysis/. | TQ22 | Draft branches need git worktrees for CRDT file isolation                                     | Technical     | P0       | Assumed (Medium confidence) | When agent creates a draft while human is on main, agent's Y.Docs need a separate file directory for persistence. Git worktrees provide this: `git worktree add .ok/worktrees/restructure`. Draft Y.Docs persist to worktree directory. Main Y.Docs persist to project directory. Hocuspocus document naming (`{branch}/{filepath}`) routes to correct Y.Docs. Lazy-load: draft Y.Docs initialized from worktree files on first access. Verification: prototype during S6/CC4 spec.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| PQ17 | Catalog file scope: eager render all frontmatter                                              | Product       | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | Catalog file at every level eagerly renders ALL frontmatter fields from direct children (articles + subfolders). No filtering, no curation at P0. At \~100 articles the root is \~5-10KB — one read for full orientation. No field filtering at P0. Configurable field exclusion/customization is a later feature. At 500+ articles, the root naturally groups by folders (each folder's articles are in their own catalog, root lists folders). Catalog file naming is OPEN (see CC6).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| TQ24 | Catalog portability principle: any coding agent at any folder depth sees the same structure   | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | If a coding agent enters a folder at n-depth, the experience should be identical to entering at the root — the agent finds a catalog file listing children with all their frontmatter, and can navigate deeper via links to child folder catalogs. Same recursive structure at every level. Works for ANY agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) without our product running — it's just files. The walkable catalog IS the portable agent interface. No MCP server required for basic navigation. Catalog file naming is OPEN (see CC6). | TQ23 | Runtime: Node.js for distribution, Bun for development (hybrid)                               | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | Node.js for `npx openknowledge` distribution (stable, universal, LTS). Bun for dev tooling (7x faster install, native TS, faster tests). Single npm package serves both — zero Bun-specific APIs needed. 8 of 9 key dependencies work in Bun; @parcel/watcher needs trustedDependencies workaround (acceptable for dev). Performance advantages (2-7x WebSocket, 3-4x startup) irrelevant at local-tool scale — bottleneck is react-docgen-typescript (10-15s, CPU-bound, runtime-agnostic). See /reports/bun-vs-node-runtime/. | TQ25 | Source toggle sync: bidirectional observer sync between Y.XmlFragment and Y.Text              | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by PR #6 (browser E2E).** `src/editor/observers.ts`: bidirectional observers between Y.XmlFragment('default') and Y.Text('source'). Observer A (tree→text): incremental diff via `diffLines`. Observer B (text→tree): parse → `updateYFragment`. Origin guards prevent loops. 22 server-side observer-sync tests + Playwright E2E coverage in `tests/e2e/sync.spec.ts`. Full sync matrix green. Toggle simplified to instant show/hide (no serialize-on-toggle). See /reports/yjs-dual-key-shimmer-analysis/, /reports/yjs-constrained-observer-sync/.                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ26 | Disk↔CRDT bridge for external editor interop (Cursor, VS Code)                                | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by PR #6 (browser E2E).** `src/server/file-watcher.ts`: @parcel/watcher with two-layer feedback prevention (content-hash + skipStoreHooks). 10 unit tests + Playwright E2E coverage in `tests/e2e/qa-scenarios.spec.ts`. External file edits appear in both WYSIWYG and source mode. See /reports/parcel-watcher-crdt-disk-bridge/. | TQ28 | Three-way merge on source toggle-back                                                         | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by init\_spike A2+A3.** When user is in source mode and agent writes via DirectConnection, both edits must survive toggle-back. Architecture: snapshot Y.Doc state on toggle-to-source. On toggle-back: diff snapshot vs user's source edits to find human changes. Detect agent-added blocks (positions beyond snapshot length). Merge: human edits applied, agent additions preserved, conflicts → human wins. Combined with A1 (agent markdown write path): agent writes are visible in source mode in real-time (Y.Doc observer → re-serialize → push to CodeMirror) AND preserved on toggle-back. See init\_spike/src/editor/three-way-merge.ts.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| TQ27 | MDX component scope: predefined registry, no imports                                          | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | **Validated by real content analysis.** agents-docs: 201 .mdx files, 846 JSX component uses, 3 top-level imports (1 build-time templating, 1 one-off custom component, 1 redundant). openbolts docs: 8 .mdx files, 2 Callout uses, zero imports. Components are resolved globally via getMDXComponents() — not imported per-file. The predefined set: \~20 components (Callout, Tabs/Tab, Steps/Step, Card/Cards, Accordion, AutoTypeTable, Image, CodeGroup, Video, Warning, Note, Tip, Files/File, Banner, SkillRule, ComparisonTable, Snippet, APIPage). All have simple string/boolean props. No expression props, no inline JSX expressions, no MDX comments in real content. This means observer sync (TQ25) handles all actual MDX patterns — the void node approach covers the real usage. See /reports/mdx-crdt-roundtrip-fidelity/ addendum Section 11.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| TQ29 | Component discovery: layered scan (built-in → project → explicit config)                      | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Three-layer discovery hierarchy for the component registry. **Layer 0 (built-in):** openknowledge ships default components (Callout, Tabs, Accordion, Card, Steps, Note/Warning/Tip) — always available, zero config. **Layer 1 (project auto-detection):** At dev server start, scan: (1) `mdx-components.tsx` (fumadocs convention) → parse imports, merge; (2) `src/components/` → scan .tsx files; (3) `.ok/components/` → scan. **Layer 2 (explicit config):** `.ok/components.ts` for manual mapping + overrides. Open sub-questions: Should auto-detection be convention-based or config-driven? How to handle fumadocs-ui library components in node\_modules (too expensive to scan all, must be explicit via mdx-components.tsx)? Layer priority when same component name appears in multiple layers (project overrides built-in)? Spike validates Layer 0 + simple scan of `src/components/`; Layers 1-2 are P0. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| TQ30 | .ok/ directory: project-level convention namespace                                | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | `.ok/` as the project-level directory for Open Knowledge config and derived artifacts. Contains: `components.ts` (explicit component registry + overrides), `component-cache.json` (react-docgen-typescript cache), future: `permissions.yaml`, `config.ts`. Analogous to `.vscode/`, `.cursor/`, `.github/`. Open sub-questions: Should this be `.ok/` or configurable? Gitignore story (config committed, cache ignored)? Relationship to fumadocs conventions (`source.config.ts`, `mdx-components.tsx`) — complement or replace?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| PQ18 | Component scope for non-fumadocs projects                                                     | Product       | P0       | Open                        | For projects that are NOT fumadocs sites (plain markdown KBs, Obsidian-style vaults): What components are available? Options: (A) built-ins only — ship with openknowledge, user writes `jsx-component` fenced code blocks, no React project needed. (B) `.ok/components/` directory — user drops .tsx files, editor discovers them, requires React/TypeScript. (C) shadcn-style install — `npx openknowledge add callout` copies component source into project, connects to S-L7 (shadcn registry). Key question: does the editor require a React/TypeScript project to render custom components, or can built-in components work in any project type? Spike: built-ins only in `src/components/`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ31 | react-docgen-typescript integration: config and gotchas                                       | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Findings from typed-component-nodes spec investigation: (1) `children` prop FILTERED OUT by default — must set `skipChildrenPropWithoutDoc: false`. (2) ReactNode type appears as `"ReactNode"` or `"React.ReactNode"` depending on import — detection must check both. (3) Use `shouldExtractLiteralValuesFromEnum: true` for union→dropdown, NOT `shouldExtractValuesFromUnion: true` (explodes ReactNode into 11 useless members). (4) \~10-15s for 75 components — cache to `.ok/component-cache.json` with mtime invalidation. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/evidence/react-docgen-typescript-behavior.md.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| XQ4  | Deterministic health layer — validators in core service, consumed by writes + editor + skills | Cross-cutting | P0       | Open                        | The product should compute deterministic health signals (orphan pages, broken links, frontmatter completeness, staleness, tag duplicates, stub pages) as an in-process validation library in the core service. Two consumption paths, same code: (1) **on every write** — per-file validators run inline with the MCP write pipeline, return warnings in tool response (\~1-5ms, don't block the write); (2) **batch health summary** — computed file (e.g., `.ok/health.json`, gitignored) with KB-wide signals, readable by any consumer. Editor shows health signals visually (orphan badges, broken-link underlines, stale indicators) without any skill running. The `/lint` reference skill (PQ14) EXTENDS this layer with LLM-powered checks (contradictions, missing cross-refs, concept gaps) — it reads the health summary first, then adds semantic analysis. Deterministic checks: orphans (from backlink index), broken links (from file tree), frontmatter completeness (structural parse), staleness (timestamp comparison of `compiled_at` vs latest timeline entry), tag fuzzy-matching, stub detection. LLM checks (skill-only): contradictions, missing cross-references, concept-without-page, semantic staleness, data gaps. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d11-reference-skill-mapping.md for the merged lint checklist from Karpathy + GBrain + obsidian-mind.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PQ19 | Compiled truth + timeline: content convention for agent-authored entries                      | Product       | P0       | Open                        | A convention (not product-enforced schema) for how reference skills author knowledge entries. Above-the-line: rewritable compiled truth (Key Judgment, State, Assessment, Open Questions). Below-the-line (after `---`): append-only timeline with structured entries (`- **YYYY-MM-DD** \| source \| @author — Summary. Confidence: level.`). Convention validated across 6 independent domains (intelligence analysis, Wikipedia, Zettelkasten, Karpathy, ByteRover, GBrain). Compilation rules (for skills): compiled truth always rewritten from timeline evidence (not from prior compiled truth — prevents circular compilation); timeline always appended, never rewritten; 5 metadata fields per timeline entry (date, source, author, type, confidence). Staleness: product can deterministically flag entries where `compiled_at` < latest timeline date (feeds XQ4 health layer). Resolve exact format at skill spec time. See /reports/compiled-truth-timeline-content-conventions/ D6.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| XQ5  | Git scaling: \~5K file soft ceiling for markdown+git KBs                                      | Cross-cutting | P0       | Assumed (Medium confidence) | GBrain's spec documents git performance degradation at 7,471 files / 2.3GB (Garry Tan's personal brain). Not a hard limit — Linux kernel has 80K+ files in git — but depends on commit frequency, file sizes, and working-tree operations. Open-knowledge's auto-commit pipeline (CC2: every 30-60s) amplifies the concern. P0 target (100-1000 articles) is comfortably within range. Scaling path: multi-KB (separate git repos per knowledge domain), not single-monolithic-KB. SQLite-backed storage (GBrain's approach) is a Later alternative for power users at scale. Document the limit in day-0 user-facing docs. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d6-garrytan-gbrain.md.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PQ20 | Markdown/MDX preview pane — design and phasing                                                | Product       | P0       | Open                        | Side panel rendering markdown/MDX output, auto-refreshes based on editor changes. Design space: (A) third mode alongside WYSIWYG and source, (B) split-pane alongside CodeMirror source view, (C) always-available side panel in any mode. Obsidian has reading view + source side-by-side. For MDX: requires runtime compilation — DynamicCodeBlock handles syntax highlighting, but full component rendering needs React in the preview pane. Connects to CC3 (rendering paths not yet designed), S2 (source toggle forward connections). Owned: Dima/Sarah.
