| PQ15 | AI-generated metadata placement                                                               | Product       | P0       | Open                        | Where does AI-generated metadata live (summaries, auto-tags, related article suggestions)? Three options: (A) same frontmatter fields as human-written (simplest but no provenance — can't tell who wrote what, regeneration risks overwriting human edits), (B) separate `ai:` namespace in frontmatter (clear provenance, product can regenerate ai: without touching human fields, human can delete ai: block and nothing breaks), (C) separate sidecar file (complete separation, article stays clean, but filesystem clutter + agent needs to know about the sidecar). Affects: index file content (include ai: fields?), permission model (can AI overwrite human frontmatter?), update\_frontmatter MCP tool design, skill conventions.                                            |
| TQ13 | CRDT: Yjs + Hocuspocus for real-time sync, complementary to git branches                      | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | After exploring 4 levels (file watcher → WebSocket → OT → CRDT), CRDT held. **Alternative CRDT stacks assessed and rejected for P0:** Automerge doesn't solve dual-view natively (CodeMirror binding doesn't understand rich text structure), 12-20 week migration, 1.7MB WASM bundle — see /reports/automerge-prosemirror-migration-assessment/. Loro has native fork/merge (genuinely differentiated for draft architecture) but ecosystem too immature: content-wipe bug in PM binding (#77), 1 production user (SchoolAI), no production sync server — see /reports/loro-ecosystem-readiness-assessment/. **Yjs v14 tested (init-spike V7): FAIL** — unified YType not realized, y-prosemirror v2 doesn't exist, ecosystem pins yjs@^13. **Decision: stay on Yjs v13 + Hocuspocus.** Revisit Loro in 12-18 months when ecosystem matures (fork/merge is the trigger). CRDT and git remain complementary. Branch merge always text-level. See /reports/crdt-branching-namespacing-prior-art/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| TQ14 | Draft isolation: git branches + CRDT per active branch                                        | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | Drafts are git branches. CRDT (Yjs) operates on whichever branch is active — document names prefixed by branch. Branch switch = `git checkout` + editor remount + Hocuspocus loads branch's Y.Docs + cache deserialize (CC6). Human CAN co-edit within a draft (same CRDT mechanism as main — if human opens a draft, CRDT syncs on that branch). Industry convergence on git worktrees for agent isolation (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor 3, Windsurf). Branch merge = git merge --squash on .md files (text-level, never CRDT-level). See /reports/claude-code-worktree-git-isolation/. | TQ15 | MCP write path: **Partially validated.** Yjs Y.Doc via Hocuspocus DirectConnection.           | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **Partially validated by init-spike V3 + agent-markdown-writes spike.** Two write paths implemented: (1) Raw Y.XmlElement construction via DirectConnection (POST /api/agent-write) — QA-confirmed: paragraph appears in browser editor. (2) Markdown text via parse→updateYFragment (POST /api/agent-write-md) — server-side validated, unifies write path with toggle-back. **Not yet browser-verified for path 2:** agent markdown writes appearing in the WYSIWYG editor in real-time. **Cursor preservation during agent writes (V3 step 5) not tested.** External writes via file watcher not yet implemented (TQ21). See init\_spike/RESULTS.md V3, init\_spike/src/server/hocuspocus-plugin.ts. | PQ16 | Merge conflict approach when applying drafts                                                  | Product       | P0       | Open                        | Three options: (A) prevent conflicts — warn when human edits a file that's in a draft, (B) section-level visual merge — show "your version / draft version / keep both" per-section, (C) main wins — human's edits preserved, draft's conflicting sections shown as "couldn't apply, view draft's version." Option C is simplest for P0 (no merge UI needed, human's work preserved, agent retries). Options B needed for teams (Later). | TQ16 | Agent edit patterns: section-level, not character-level                                       | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Research confirmed: agents apply changes via exactly two operations — full file write and string replacement. Both section-level. 8/11 agents use string replacement as primary. Claude Code enforces read-before-write and exact match. This is factual — affects TQ13 (CRDT merge granularity question). See /reports/ai-coding-agent-tool-surfaces/.                                                                                                    |
| TQ17 | JS regex on CRDT content: 2-8ms at 1000 files (faster than ripgrep)                           | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual. V8 Irregexp JIT-compiled regex on in-memory strings: 2-8ms for 1000 files / 5MB. ripgrep from disk: 22ms (bottlenecked by process spawn + I/O). At our scale, in-memory JS regex is FASTER than ripgrep because ripgrep's advantages (SIMD, parallelism, mmap) solve I/O problems that don't exist when data is in CRDT memory. No WASM, no workers needed. Pre-split lines on CRDT update is the one optimization that matters (1.2-1.7x). Bun vs Node: no consistent regex winner. See /reports/orama-vs-ripgrep-indexed-grep/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| TQ18 | Index files and regex are complementary, not competing                                        | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual, updated. P0 navigation: walkable index.md files for orientation/discovery (find articles ABOUT a topic via hierarchical browsing). Regex for exact pattern matching (find lines CONTAINING a pattern). At 100-1000 files, no search engine needed — index files + JS regex on CRDT content (2-8ms) cover both agent needs. BM25/vector search deferred to Next. For agents: index files serve Layers 1-2 (orientation + discovery), grep serves Layer 3 (targeted extraction). Research: /reports/orama-vs-ripgrep-indexed-grep/ D7, /reports/kb-index-navigation-patterns-for-agents/ D9.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ19 | structuredContent not viable for enrichment (most hosts ignore it)                            | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | Factual. MCP structuredContent (added June 2025 spec) is ignored by Claude Code (issue #4427, closed "not planned"), Cursor (confirmed by team), Windsurf, n8n. Only ChatGPT Apps reads it. Enrichment MUST be in the text `content` field. Our command implementations embed metadata directly in string output (frontmatter already in files, backlinks as footer, titles/tags inline with grep results). No dual-format responses needed. See research on structuredContent support. Isolated git index via GIT\_INDEX\_FILE env var prevents polluting developer staging area. Subprocess spawn negligible at 30s commit intervals. See init\_spike/src/server/persistence.ts. | TQ21 | just-bash as potential MCP implementation layer                                               | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Vercel Labs' just-bash: TypeScript virtual bash with pluggable IFileSystem. 100+ Unix commands reimplemented. Mintlify ChromaFs built on it (30K+ daily conversations, \~100ms p90). A YjsFileSystem backend is viable (\~8 methods). Enables: `mcp__openkb__exec(command)` as single MCP tool, whitelisted commands, composable pipelines, enriched output. Pattern validated by just-bash-mcp (community). Connects to XQ1 Approach B. Not yet decided — open product/technical question. See /reports/just-bash-virtual-filesystem-analysis/. | TQ22 | Draft branches need git worktrees for CRDT file isolation                                     | Technical     | P0       | Assumed (Medium confidence) | When agent creates a draft while human is on main, agent's Y.Docs need a separate file directory for persistence. Git worktrees provide this: `git worktree add .ok/worktrees/restructure`. Draft Y.Docs persist to worktree directory. Main Y.Docs persist to project directory. Hocuspocus document naming (`{branch}/{filepath}`) routes to correct Y.Docs. Lazy-load: draft Y.Docs initialized from worktree files on first access. Verification: prototype during S6/CC4 spec. | PQ17 | Catalog file scope: eager render all frontmatter                                              | Product       | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | Catalog file at every level eagerly renders ALL frontmatter fields from direct children (articles + subfolders). No filtering, no curation at P0. At \~100 articles the root is \~5-10KB — one read for full orientation. No field filtering at P0. Configurable field exclusion/customization is a later feature. At 500+ articles, the root naturally groups by folders (each folder's articles are in their own catalog, root lists folders). Catalog file naming is OPEN (see CC6). | TQ24 | Catalog portability principle: any coding agent at any folder depth sees the same structure   | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | If a coding agent enters a folder at n-depth, the experience should be identical to entering at the root — the agent finds a catalog file listing children with all their frontmatter, and can navigate deeper via links to child folder catalogs. Same recursive structure at every level. Works for ANY agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) without our product running — it's just files. The walkable catalog IS the portable agent interface. No MCP server required for basic navigation. Catalog file naming is OPEN (see CC6). | TQ23 | Runtime: Node.js for distribution, Bun for development (hybrid)                               | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | Node.js for `npx openknowledge` distribution (stable, universal, LTS). Bun for dev tooling (7x faster install, native TS, faster tests). Single npm package serves both — zero Bun-specific APIs needed. 8 of 9 key dependencies work in Bun; @parcel/watcher needs trustedDependencies workaround (acceptable for dev). Performance advantages (2-7x WebSocket, 3-4x startup) irrelevant at local-tool scale — bottleneck is react-docgen-typescript (10-15s, CPU-bound, runtime-agnostic). See /reports/bun-vs-node-runtime/. | TQ25 | Source toggle sync: bidirectional observer sync between Y.XmlFragment and Y.Text              | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by PR #6 (browser E2E).** `src/editor/observers.ts`: bidirectional observers between Y.XmlFragment('default') and Y.Text('source'). Observer A (tree→text): incremental diff via `diffLines`. Observer B (text→tree): parse → `updateYFragment`. Origin guards prevent loops. 22 server-side observer-sync tests + Playwright E2E coverage in `tests/e2e/sync.spec.ts`. Full sync matrix green. Toggle simplified to instant show/hide (no serialize-on-toggle). See /reports/yjs-dual-key-shimmer-analysis/, /reports/yjs-constrained-observer-sync/.                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ26 | Disk↔CRDT bridge for external editor interop (Cursor, VS Code)                                | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by PR #6 (browser E2E).** `src/server/file-watcher.ts`: @parcel/watcher with two-layer feedback prevention (content-hash + skipStoreHooks). 10 unit tests + Playwright E2E coverage in `tests/e2e/qa-scenarios.spec.ts`. External file edits appear in both WYSIWYG and source mode. See /reports/parcel-watcher-crdt-disk-bridge/. | TQ28 | Three-way merge on source toggle-back                                                         | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Locked)            | **CONFIRMED by init\_spike A2+A3.** When user is in source mode and agent writes via DirectConnection, both edits must survive toggle-back. Architecture: snapshot Y.Doc state on toggle-to-source. On toggle-back: diff snapshot vs user's source edits to find human changes. Detect agent-added blocks (positions beyond snapshot length). Merge: human edits applied, agent additions preserved, conflicts → human wins. Combined with A1 (agent markdown write path): agent writes are visible in source mode in real-time (Y.Doc observer → re-serialize → push to CodeMirror) AND preserved on toggle-back. See init\_spike/src/editor/three-way-merge.ts.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| TQ27 | MDX component scope: predefined registry, no imports                                          | Technical     | P0       | Decided (Directed)          | **Validated by real content analysis.** agents-docs: 201 .mdx files, 846 JSX component uses, 3 top-level imports (1 build-time templating, 1 one-off custom component, 1 redundant). openbolts docs: 8 .mdx files, 2 Callout uses, zero imports. Components are resolved globally via getMDXComponents() — not imported per-file. The predefined set: \~20 components (Callout, Tabs/Tab, Steps/Step, Card/Cards, Accordion, AutoTypeTable, Image, CodeGroup, Video, Warning, Note, Tip, Files/File, Banner, SkillRule, ComparisonTable, Snippet, APIPage). All have simple string/boolean props. No expression props, no inline JSX expressions, no MDX comments in real content. This means observer sync (TQ25) handles all actual MDX patterns — the void node approach covers the real usage. See /reports/mdx-crdt-roundtrip-fidelity/ addendum Section 11.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| TQ29 | Component discovery: layered scan (built-in → project → explicit config)                      | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Three-layer discovery hierarchy for the component registry. **Layer 0 (built-in):** openknowledge ships default components (Callout, Tabs, Accordion, Card, Steps, Note/Warning/Tip) — always available, zero config. **Layer 1 (project auto-detection):** At dev server start, scan: (1) `mdx-components.tsx` (fumadocs convention) → parse imports, merge; (2) `src/components/` → scan .tsx files; (3) `.ok/components/` → scan. **Layer 2 (explicit config):** `.ok/components.ts` for manual mapping + overrides. Open sub-questions: Should auto-detection be convention-based or config-driven? How to handle fumadocs-ui library components in node\_modules (too expensive to scan all, must be explicit via mdx-components.tsx)? Layer priority when same component name appears in multiple layers (project overrides built-in)? Spike validates Layer 0 + simple scan of `src/components/`; Layers 1-2 are P0. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| TQ30 | .ok/ directory: project-level convention namespace                                | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | `.ok/` as the project-level directory for Open Knowledge config and derived artifacts. Contains: `components.ts` (explicit component registry + overrides), `component-cache.json` (react-docgen-typescript cache), future: `permissions.yaml`, `config.ts`. Analogous to `.vscode/`, `.cursor/`, `.github/`. Open sub-questions: Should this be `.ok/` or configurable? Gitignore story (config committed, cache ignored)? Relationship to fumadocs conventions (`source.config.ts`, `mdx-components.tsx`) — complement or replace?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| PQ18 | Component scope for non-fumadocs projects                                                     | Product       | P0       | Open                        | For projects that are NOT fumadocs sites (plain markdown KBs, Obsidian-style vaults): What components are available? Options: (A) built-ins only — ship with openknowledge, user writes `jsx-component` fenced code blocks, no React project needed. (B) `.ok/components/` directory — user drops .tsx files, editor discovers them, requires React/TypeScript. (C) shadcn-style install — `npx openknowledge add callout` copies component source into project, connects to S-L7 (shadcn registry). Key question: does the editor require a React/TypeScript project to render custom components, or can built-in components work in any project type? Spike: built-ins only in `src/components/`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| TQ31 | react-docgen-typescript integration: config and gotchas                                       | Technical     | P0       | Open                        | Findings from typed-component-nodes spec investigation: (1) `children` prop FILTERED OUT by default — must set `skipChildrenPropWithoutDoc: false`. (2) ReactNode type appears as `"ReactNode"` or `"React.ReactNode"` depending on import — detection must check both. (3) Use `shouldExtractLiteralValuesFromEnum: true` for union→dropdown, NOT `shouldExtractValuesFromUnion: true` (explodes ReactNode into 11 useless members). (4) \~10-15s for 75 components — cache to `.ok/component-cache.json` with mtime invalidation. See specs/2026-04-08-typed-component-nodes/evidence/react-docgen-typescript-behavior.md.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| XQ4  | Deterministic health layer — validators in core service, consumed by writes + editor + skills | Cross-cutting | P0       | Open                        | The product should compute deterministic health signals (orphan pages, broken links, frontmatter completeness, staleness, tag duplicates, stub pages) as an in-process validation library in the core service. Two consumption paths, same code: (1) **on every write** — per-file validators run inline with the MCP write pipeline, return warnings in tool response (\~1-5ms, don't block the write); (2) **batch health summary** — computed file (e.g., `.ok/health.json`, gitignored) with KB-wide signals, readable by any consumer. Editor shows health signals visually (orphan badges, broken-link underlines, stale indicators) without any skill running. The `/lint` reference skill (PQ14) EXTENDS this layer with LLM-powered checks (contradictions, missing cross-refs, concept gaps) — it reads the health summary first, then adds semantic analysis. Deterministic checks: orphans (from backlink index), broken links (from file tree), frontmatter completeness (structural parse), staleness (timestamp comparison of `compiled_at` vs latest timeline entry), tag fuzzy-matching, stub detection. LLM checks (skill-only): contradictions, missing cross-references, concept-without-page, semantic staleness, data gaps. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d11-reference-skill-mapping.md for the merged lint checklist from Karpathy + GBrain + obsidian-mind.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PQ19 | Compiled truth + timeline: content convention for agent-authored entries                      | Product       | P0       | Open                        | A convention (not product-enforced schema) for how reference skills author knowledge entries. Above-the-line: rewritable compiled truth (Key Judgment, State, Assessment, Open Questions). Below-the-line (after `---`): append-only timeline with structured entries (`- **YYYY-MM-DD** \| source \| @author — Summary. Confidence: level.`). Convention validated across 6 independent domains (intelligence analysis, Wikipedia, Zettelkasten, Karpathy, ByteRover, GBrain). Compilation rules (for skills): compiled truth always rewritten from timeline evidence (not from prior compiled truth — prevents circular compilation); timeline always appended, never rewritten; 5 metadata fields per timeline entry (date, source, author, type, confidence). Staleness: product can deterministically flag entries where `compiled_at` < latest timeline date (feeds XQ4 health layer). Resolve exact format at skill spec time. See /reports/compiled-truth-timeline-content-conventions/ D6.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| XQ5  | Git scaling: \~5K file soft ceiling for markdown+git KBs                                      | Cross-cutting | P0       | Assumed (Medium confidence) | GBrain's spec documents git performance degradation at 7,471 files / 2.3GB (Garry Tan's personal brain). Not a hard limit — Linux kernel has 80K+ files in git — but depends on commit frequency, file sizes, and working-tree operations. Open-knowledge's auto-commit pipeline (CC2: every 30-60s) amplifies the concern. P0 target (100-1000 articles) is comfortably within range. Scaling path: multi-KB (separate git repos per knowledge domain), not single-monolithic-KB. SQLite-backed storage (GBrain's approach) is a Later alternative for power users at scale. Document the limit in day-0 user-facing docs. See /reports/open-knowledge-prior-art-eight-sources/evidence/d6-garrytan-gbrain.md.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PQ20 | Markdown/MDX preview pane — design and phasing                                                | Product       | P0       | Open                        | Side panel rendering markdown/MDX output, auto-refreshes based on editor changes. Design space: (A) third mode alongside WYSIWYG and source, (B) split-pane alongside CodeMirror source view, (C) always-available side panel in any mode. Obsidian has reading view + source side-by-side. For MDX: requires runtime compilation — DynamicCodeBlock handles syntax highlighting, but full component rendering needs React in the preview pane. Connects to CC3 (rendering paths not yet designed), S2 (source toggle forward connections). Owned: Dima/Sarah.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| PQ21 | Skill auto-registration mechanics — cross-platform                                            | Product       | P0       | Open                        | When a user runs `npx openknowledge init`, how do reference skills become available to their agent? Must work cross-platform (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Cowork). Options: (A) MCP `instructions` field lists available skills on connect — agent discovers via MCP, (B) AGENTS.md in project root documents skills — any agent reads file, (C) modify agent config (e.g., `.claude/settings.json`) to register skill commands — Claude Code only, not portable, (D) skills are just .md files in the project — agent finds via catalog files (CC6), no registration needed. Option D is simplest and most portable but requires agents to know the SKILL.md convention. Option A uses existing MCP infrastructure. CC7 principle: prefer MCP/files over hooks. Connects to PQ14 (distribution), CC7 (agent DevX), CC5 (onboarding). Owned: Tim.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| PQ22 | Raw artifact lifecycle — where do ingested sources land?                                      | Product       | P0       | Open                        | When an agent ingests a source (URL, PDF, pasted text), where does the raw artifact live? Options: (A) `sources/` or `raw/` convention folder — raw artifacts preserved, compiled articles reference them, (B) raw artifacts are transient — skill processes source and only the compiled article persists, (C) raw artifacts in frontmatter/metadata — source URL and extraction date recorded, raw text discarded. Rabbit hole #4 caution applies (don't over-engineer conventions — let them emerge). The compiled truth + timeline convention (PQ19) covers the *output* format; this covers the *input* lifecycle. Key question: do raw artifacts have independent value (searchable, browsable) or are they only useful during compilation? Connects to PQ6 (ingest), PQ13 (Karpathy workflow), PQ14 (reference skills). Owned: Mike.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| PQ23 | Multi-project support — switching between KBs                                                 | Product       | P0       | Open                        | How does the editor handle multiple knowledge bases? Options: (A) one instance per project — user runs `npx openknowledge` in each project directory, switches via browser tabs, (B) project switcher in UI — editor lists known projects, user selects, server switches context, (C) workspace model — multiple projects open simultaneously with a sidebar per project. P0 is likely (A) — one instance, one project. Project switcher (B) is a Next feature. The MCP server already scopes to one project. XQ5 mentions "multi-KB (separate git repos)" as the scaling path. Connects to CC5 (onboarding), CC3 (web UI shell), XQ6 (deployment model). Owned: Miles.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| XQ6  | KB deployment model — git relationship and root directory resolution                          | Cross-cutting | P0       | Open                        | Two coupled questions: (1) **Git relationship:** Is the KB its own git repo, a subdirectory of a parent project, or a git submodule? Auto-persistence (CC2) assumes git operations — auto-commits to a shared repo could conflict with the parent project's workflow. Isolated git index (TQ20) mitigates but doesn't eliminate. (2) **Root resolution:** How does `npx openknowledge` find the KB root? Options: (A) walk up from cwd looking for `.ok/` marker, (B) explicit config (`--root` flag or env var), (C) assume cwd is root. If KB is a subdirectory of a monorepo, (A) is critical. **Three deployment scenarios to support:** (i) standalone KB repo (`~/knowledge/` — simplest, CC2 works unmodified), (ii) subdirectory of code repo (`myproject/docs/` — CC2 must scope git commits to subtree, or use a separate orphan branch), (iii) git submodule (`myproject/knowledge/` pointing to separate repo — cleanest isolation, most git complexity for users). P0 should support (i) and (ii). (iii) is Later. Connects to CC2, CC5, TQ20, TQ30, PQ23. Owned: Miles.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

## Cross-cutting concerns

### CC1: Real-time sync — Yjs CRDT + Hocuspocus, complementary to git branches (TQ13 resolved)

**The CRDT is the source of truth during editing. Files on disk are projections.**
Y.Docs hold the live content. .md files are serialized projections (2-10s behind via persistence hooks). MCP tools read from CRDT (fresh), not from disk (stale). Index.md files, backlink maps, and (when added) search indexes are all derived from CRDT content. This is structurally the same pattern as Mintlify's ChromaFs (content in a store, files as derived views) — just with Yjs instead of Chroma.

**Two complementary layers:**

- **CRDT (Yjs + Hocuspocus):** Real-time sync WITHIN the active branch. Editor ↔ MCP agent. Per-origin undo, in-document cursor, presence.
- **Git branches:** Isolation BETWEEN branches. Drafts, proposals, merge, history.

They don't interfere. Branch merge is ALWAYS text-level (git merge on .md files), NEVER CRDT-level (Yjs interleaves diverged docs). The CRDT operates on whichever branch is active.

**Hocuspocus document naming IS the branching mechanism.** Document name = `{branch}/{filepath}`. Different name = different Y.Doc. Main: `main/articles/deploy.md`. Draft: `draft:restructure/articles/deploy.md`. Zero library changes — Hocuspocus already manages Y.Docs by name.

**Branch switch = editor remount:**

1. Serialize current Y.Docs (Hocuspocus persistence)
2. `git checkout` target branch (files switch atomically)
3. React key change → editor remounts → new y-prosemirror binding
4. Hocuspocus loads target branch's Y.Docs (from storage or from .md files)
5. Derived data: deserialize from per-branch cache (CC6)

**Three write paths:**

- **Primary (MCP):** Agent → `mcp__openkb__write/edit` → Hocuspocus DirectConnection → Y.Doc → propagates to editor instantly. Permission-checked. Per-origin undo via trackedOrigins.
- **Human in editor:** ProseMirror → y-prosemirror → Y.Doc → Hocuspocus persistence → disk (debounced 2-10s) → git (30-60s).
- **Fallback (external):** VS Code / Claude native tools write to disk → @parcel/watcher (25-50ms) → read file, diff → apply to Y.Doc → editor updates. Three-way merge: human wins on overlap. Never a "file changed on disk" dialog.

**CRDT bindings (validated by init\_spike + PR #6):**

- **WYSIWYG mode:** y-prosemirror binds TipTap to Y.XmlFragment('default'). Block-level CRDT. Void nodes atomic. Full real-time sync. **CONFIRMED — browser E2E.**
- **Source mode (TQ25 observer sync):** Bidirectional observers between Y.XmlFragment and Y.Text('source'). CodeMirror binds to Y.Text via y-codemirror.next. WYSIWYG edits → serialize → incremental diff → Y.Text. Source edits → parse → updateYFragment. Transaction origin guards prevent loops. **CONFIRMED — 22 server-side observer-sync tests + Playwright E2E via `tests/e2e/sync.spec.ts` (PR #6).** V4b (serialize-on-toggle) is the fallback; V4b three-way merge (TQ28) kept as utility for future disk bridge use.
- Mini CodeMirror in void nodes: edit string attribute on parent node, no separate binding.

**Cross-mode sync matrix (E2E verified by PR #6, 24 Playwright tests across sync.spec.ts + qa-scenarios.spec.ts):**

```
                WYSIWYG   Source   Disk   Agent
WYSIWYG  →       ✅        ✅      ✅      ✅
Source   →       ✅        ✅      ✅      ✅
Disk     →       ✅        ✅      —       —
Agent    →       ✅        ✅      ✅      —
```

All critical paths green. Toggle is instant show/hide (no serialize-on-toggle). Source↔Source multi-tab sync works via shared Y.Text CRDT. Disk bridge (@parcel/watcher) delivers external edits to both modes.

**P0:** Hocuspocus embedded in Vite dev server (TQ12 — research recommends Vite). **Cloud (Later):** Hocuspocus hosted remotely. Editor + MCP tools unchanged — they talk to Hocuspocus either way.

### CC2: Auto-persistence (three layers, each serving a different concern)

Three layers with different frequencies because they serve different purposes:

**Layer 1: CRDT → filesystem (2-10s) — "your data is safe"**
Hocuspocus `onStoreDocument` hook with debounce (2s quiet / 10s max). Writes Yjs binary to disk. Crash recovery — if the process dies, at most 10s of edits lost. This is NOT git. Not human-readable. Just fast binary persistence.

**Layer 2: Filesystem → git auto-commit (30-60s idle-debounced) — "your history is browsable"**
Hocuspocus `afterStoreDocument` hook triggers git pipeline. Serializes CRDT → markdown files → git commit to WIP refs (invisible). 30s after you stop editing, OR 60s of continuous editing (whichever first). Why not faster: git degrades at high commit rates (30K commits = 11s for `git log` without optimization), and history becomes unbrowsable noise. Why not slower: 30-60s is enough granularity to rewind to "before Claude's last edit." Data safety is already handled by Layer 1 — this layer is for time travel, not crash recovery.

**Layer 3: Named checkpoints (user-initiated) — "this is a meaningful milestone"**
User clicks "Save Version." Squash-merge from WIP refs to main branch. Single clean commit with description. Annotated git tag. These are the prominent entries in the version timeline. Everything between checkpoints collapses in the UI.

Same pipeline applies to drafts — Layer 2 writes to `refs/drafts/<name>` instead of `refs/wip/<writer>/main`. When draft is applied, squash-merge creates one Layer 3 checkpoint on main.

No save button. No git terminology. Per-writer WIP refs for attribution. Pipeline uses Hocuspocus hooks (not filesystem watchers — race conditions with multi-file writes). See evidence/auto-persistence-architecture.md. Transferred from OpenDesign Reports 46 with evidence from Figma, v0, Lovable, Replit, Cline, Google Docs commit frequency strategies.

### CC3: Web UI shell

The application frame: sidebar/file tree, top bar, settings, routing between views. Built once as the foundation. Technology: React web app on Vite dev server (TQ12, locked — validated by init-spike V2, browser-confirmed).

Touches: S1 (editor pane), S2 (source toggle within editor pane), S3 (sidebar/file tree navigation), S4 (MCP status indicator), S5 (presence indicators in top bar + sidebar), S6 (version timeline panel, draft panel). Constrains: all stories share layout, routing, and theming.

Rendering paths not yet designed (resolve during /spec): MDX live preview compilation (for CodeMirror + preview pane — see PQ20), sidebar data source (CRDT doc list vs filesystem), search results UI, version timeline UI, draft review/diff UI.

### CC4: Editing contexts — one code path, parameterized by git branch

Three contexts: Main (live state), Draft (isolated workspace), Proposal (review-gated draft). Each is a **git branch**. The editor, MCP tools, sync layer, persistence pipeline, and derived data all work **the same regardless of which branch is active**. Git determines which files. Everything else follows.

**Context switch = `git checkout` + cache deserialize (CC6).** All .md files + catalog files switch atomically (git). Backlinks load from per-branch cache. Editor reloads. MCP tools read from new branch's files. One operation, everything consistent.

**Which context an agent writes to is determined by its resolved permission** (Zanzibar model — PQ7, PQ9):

- Agent has `editor` or `maintainer` → writes to current branch (main or draft)
- Agent has `proposer` → MCP server creates a draft branch automatically
- Skill explicitly calls `create_draft()` → new branch regardless of permission

**The MCP tools don't know about branches.** They read/write files. Git already switched the files. `mcp__openkb__read("articles/deploy.md")` returns the draft version when the draft branch is checked out — no branch parameter needed. Write operations commit to whichever branch is active.

Touches: S1 (same editor on any branch), S2 (source toggle on any branch), S4 (MCP tools parameterized by active branch), S5 (presence on active branch), S6 (persistence pipeline parameterized by branch target).

Constraints: agents produce batch rewrites → suggest mode not needed (PQ11). P0 default permissions are PQ12 (open). See evidence/permission-model-zanzibar.md and evidence/editing-context-design.md.

### CC6: Derived data — "everything branchable"

Content changes produce derived data. The architectural principle: **everything that matters is either a file in git (switches with branches atomically) or a per-branch cache (regenerable from files, fast to deserialize).** Branching is free — `git checkout draft` switches all files + index.md files atomically. Cached data deserializes in milliseconds. No recomputation on branch switch.

**Committed to git (switches with branches):**

1. **Catalog files (per-folder, fully computed)** — auto-maintained catalog file at every folder, including the project root. Core requirements (decided):

   - **Recursive, uniform structure:** same format at every level. Agent enters at any folder depth and sees the same shape (TQ24 portability principle).
   - **Eager render all frontmatter:** each child file entry shows ALL its frontmatter fields. No filtering at P0 — every frontmatter field renders. Configurable exclusions (e.g., by prefix or field name) are a later feature.
   - **Folder entries show folder metadata + navigation link:** child folders listed with their title, description (from folder metadata source), computed child counts, and a link to the child folder's own catalog file for deeper navigation.
   - **Fully computed, deterministic:** generated from folder metadata + children's frontmatter + file structure. No LLM. Delete all → regenerate identically from inputs.
   - **Write-protected:** MCP `write_file`/`edit_file` reject writes to catalog files. Editor marks read-only. External edits overwritten on next content change (same `onStoreDocument` pipeline).
   - **Content-type agnostic:** works for articles (`title`/`description`), SKILL.md files (`name`/`description`), MDX component docs, or any file with frontmatter. Product handles the mapping per content type.
   - **On draft branches:** catalog files reflect the draft's content — git handles atomically.

   **Catalog file naming — OPEN.** Candidates: `llms.txt` (agent-discoverable, llms.txt spec compatible, but `.txt` can't carry YAML frontmatter), `_index.md` (Hugo precedent for list pages, `.md` fits frontmatter, but agents don't auto-discover it), `CATALOGUE.md` (descriptive, but no ecosystem precedent), `INDEX.md` (team preference — uppercase distinguishes from Fumadocs' `index.md`, `.md` format supports frontmatter, familiar convention). The naming decision is constrained by: (a) must not collide with Fumadocs' `index.md` (content page), (b) must be portable (readable by any agent without our product), (c) format must support the llms.txt spec for zero-work publishing bridge at S-L2. See analysis in this session's conversation.

   **Folder metadata — input, not output.** Folder-level metadata (title, description, icon, child ordering, custom fields) lives in a separate human/agent-authored file, NOT in the catalog file. The catalog file is fully computed output; folder metadata is input. Candidates: `meta.json`/`meta.yaml` (Fumadocs convention — we read/respect this format where present), or a field within a broader config. Agent writes folder metadata via MCP tools (e.g., `update_frontmatter` tool works on both files and folders — if path is a folder, writes to the metadata file internally). Schema is open (accepts arbitrary fields). Precedence: folder metadata file > index.md frontmatter > folder name. See /reports/fumadocs-stack-reusability-deep-analysis/ D12. Fumadocs' `llms()` catalog generator is coupled to their PageTree — not importable. Our catalog generator is \~35 lines built from scratch (different data source: CRDT + folder metadata). The remarkLLMs + stringifier pipeline (\~375 lines, zero coupling) IS importable — used for article content rendering (MDX → clean markdown for agents), not catalog generation. See D11.

   **Frontmatter is open/flexible** — the product recognizes specific fields (title, description, tags, name for skills) but accepts arbitrary additional fields. No rigid schema enforcement. Different content types have different frontmatter shapes. `description` is the single most important field for agent discoverability — feeds catalog entries across all content types. Recommended (not required) — reference skills should always populate it as a convention. Articles without description still appear in catalogs but with less navigational scent. Product does NOT auto-generate descriptions at P0 (no LLM in core).
